error_signature: JSON parameter needs to be valid JSON
title: Invalid JSON parameter in HTTP Node
symptom: When using the HTTP node with a stringified JSON parameter, the request fails with an error indicating the JSON parameter is invalid. However, manually replacing the string with a valid JSON string works.
not_this_if: Using older versions of n8n where the issue does not occur
root_cause: The issue arises from the way certain characters, like backslashes, are handled in the latest versions of n8n. Stringifying a JSON object with problematic characters can lead to invalid JSON, causing the HTTP request to fail.
fix_steps: Identify and remove problematic characters (e.g., double backslashes) from the JSON string.
Ensure the JSON string is valid before using it in the HTTP node.
agent_instruction: Clean the JSON string by removing any problematic characters before using it in the HTTP node.
